title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, Draining, and Recovery · Compiler Case: Contract Runtime Generation
summary: Documentation differs from runtime, examples contain secrets, additive changes break strict clients, storage contracts before old revisions drain, and rollback cannot restore invariants. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts contract runtime generation increment in versioned API contract and production release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose a source of truth and gate generated clients, runtime validation, examples, operation IDs, security behavior, unknown fields, and spec-to-service drift.
Describe operations, parameters, request bodies, responses, schemas, security, callbacks, webhooks, examples, links, servers, and reusable components in OpenAPI 3.2.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Contract Runtime Generation · Theory Contract Runtime Generation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9452 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate one contract-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and recoverable release; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then rotate a signing key while an old valid session remains within policy. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
